title: Anthropic hits $30B revenue run-rate as Amodei claims 80x growth in a year
subtitle: The Claude maker disclosed Q1 2026 numbers showing run-rate revenue up from roughly $9B at end of 2025 — driven mostly by Claude Code and enterprise deployments.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei disclosed Q1 2026 numbers showing the company at a roughly $30 billion annualized revenue run-rate, up from about $9 billion at the end of 2025. The trajectory he laid out: $87 million run-rate in January 2024, $1 billion by December 2024, $9 billion at end of 2025, $14 billion in February 2026, $19 billion in March, $30 billion by April. He described the year-over-year jump as 80x and called the curve 'crazy.'

What changed is the mix. The bulk of growth is enterprise: more than 1,000 customers are now spending over $1 million per year with Anthropic, a number that doubled in under two months. Claude Code — Anthropic's agentic coding product — crossed $1 billion in annualized revenue within six months of public launch, by several measures the fastest software product to that figure on record.

The comparison to OpenAI is instructive but easy to misread. OpenAI is at roughly $2 billion in monthly revenue ($24B annualized) and growing in consumer; Anthropic's lead is concentrated in coding and regulated-industry enterprise where switching costs are high. Anthropic also reports training its frontier models for roughly a quarter of what OpenAI spends per generation, which — if it holds — compounds into a margin gap that is hard to close from behind.

For learners: in this phase of the AI market, the leaderboard is set by who pays, not by who has the smartest model on a benchmark. Watch enterprise revenue and seat counts; they are a cleaner signal of which products survive a downturn. The benchmark scores are entertainment; the run-rate is the score.

sources.title: Anthropic says it hit a $30 billion revenue run rate after 'crazy' 80x growth — VentureBeat
